name: Makaha Beach
tagline: World-class surf history and world-class sunsets — the Waianae coast's crown jewel.
bestTime: Summer for calm swimming and snorkeling; winter for big wave watching (stay out of water); sunset year-round
talkStory: Makaha means 'fierce' or 'savage' in Hawaiian, and the wave here lives up to it. This was the epicenter of big-wave surfing before the North Shore took over in the 1960s — the Makaha International contest, started in 1954, was the sport's first world championship. Buffalo Keaulana's Big Board Surfing Classic every March is a celebration of old-school Hawaiian surf culture with tandem surfing and canoe races. Summer brings crystal-clear snorkeling with turtles; winter brings 20-foot waves and a fierce rip current. Respect the locals — this is their home break.
